message:
test(fcp-core): pin EvictionPolicy externally-tagged serde shape (flywheel_connectors-x2yxk)

Bead asks for RetainedSnapshot serde tag. fcp-core has no type
literally named RetainedSnapshot; the retention surface splits
across EvictionPolicy (object.rs:215, also re-exported as
RetentionClass) — the 3-variant tagged enum that decides retention
for stored objects (Pinned / Lease / Ephemeral) — and
ConnectorStateSnapshot (the actual snapshot type, already covered
by connector_state_golden_vectors.rs). Existing
eviction_policy_display.rs pins Display + equality but NOT the
serde tag.

Coverage:
- Externally-tagged JSON form per variant: Pinned → "Pinned" bare
  string, Lease { expires_at } → {"Lease":{"expires_at":<u64>}},
  Ephemeral → "Ephemeral"
- JSON round-trip per variant including Lease nested fields
- CBOR round-trip per variant
- CBOR encoding shape: unit variants as Text, struct variant as
  single-key Map with inner "expires_at" Integer
- Lease expires_at round-trips at boundaries (0 and u64::MAX)
- RetentionClass alias serializes byte-for-byte identically to
  EvictionPolicy (it's a type alias not a newtype)
- StorageMeta wrapping preserves the externally-tagged retention
  shape verbatim inside the `retention` field
- PascalCase is canonical: snake_case unit-variant tokens AND
  snake_case Lease outer keys rejected — drift sentinel for any
  future #[serde(rename_all = ...)] swap
- Unknown variants rejected

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
